tickets.title: [TKT-0294] Fix db-write.sh PG schema mismatch — merge extras into metadata JSONB

metadata.resolution: Option B implemented. db-write.sh now queries PG schema, routes known columns to INSERT, and packs unknown columns into metadata JSONB. Verified: basic columns (ticket.sh pattern), extended fields (sprint/description/etc → metadata), and edge case (id only).
metadata.description: db-write.sh fails PG writes when callers send fields not matching the PG column schema (e.g. sprint, description, resolution). The script generates INSERT with all fields as columns, but PG only has a subset. Unknown columns must be merged into the metadata JSONB column. Fix: modify db-write.sh to query PG schema, separate known columns from unknown, and pack unknowns into metadata.

tickets.title: [TKT-0295] PG Design-to-Implementation Audit — Atlas + Thrawn Joint Review

metadata.resolution: Audit complete. Atlas and Thrawn reports delivered at docs/deliverables/. 7 remediation items: TKT-0298 through TKT-0304.

tickets.title: [TKT-0296] Fix Journal Writer — Real-time append architecture, eliminate session window dependency

metadata.description: Journal writer is broken since ~May 19. Root cause: incremental writer (30-min cron) uses sessions_history(limit=50) to reconstruct entries, but session windows exceed 200+ messages in active days. Earlier entries fall outside the 50-message window and are permanently lost. Result: journals dropped from ~40kB/day to ~2kB/day (90% loss). Blog also broken (cascade failure — reads incomplete journal). Fix: redesign to real-time append architecture where the main session writes journal entries inline during conversation, not as a post-hoc reconstruction. EOD finalizer only adds header + business stream + cost report.

tickets.title: [TKT-0297] Redesign and Redevelop AKB Backlog — Clean Reliable PG-to-Notion Sync

metadata.description: Redesign AKB Backlog PG→Notion sync from scratch. Current: 642 lines across 3 scripts (ticket.sh notion-sync, notion-sync-audit.sh, notion-sync-fix.sh) with constant PG→JSON→Notion drift. New architecture: PG is SSOT. ticket.sh reads from db-read.sh (not JSON file). New pg-to-notion-sync.sh does idempotent PG→Notion sync. Deprecate old audit/fix scripts. Atoms: (1) Audit current drift, (2) Rewrite ticket.sh read path to PG primary, (3) Create pg-to-notion-sync.sh, (4) Wire ticket.sh close/create to trigger sync, (5) Archive old scripts, (6) Update AKB sync cron, (7) Regression test, (8) Close. DB IDs: Backlog=34dc1829-53ff-814b-8257-d3a3bf351d44, Auto-Heal=364c1829-53ff-81c0-9dbd-ff2c907d1a6b, Archive=364c1829-53ff-818e-a783-ebafcb6a9880.

tickets.title: TKT-0330: Ticket & CHG Numbering Architecture — PG Sequences + Atomic Increment

metadata.body:
## Problem
Ticket and CHG numbering relies on manual assignment. TKT-0329 was created with a long-form descriptive ID because no validation rejected it. CHG numbering scans a flat markdown file with regex — not atomic, not DB-backed.

## AC1: TKT Auto-Increment via PG Sequence
- Create PG sequence `ticket_seq` starting from current max + 1
- `ticket.sh create` auto-generates TKT-NNNN using `nextval()` instead of requiring manual ID
- Accept optional explicit ID override (for backfills/migrations)
- Validate TKT-NNNN format — reject non-conforming IDs
- Collision-safe: PG handles atomicity

## AC2: CHG Auto-Increment via PG Sequence + state_changelog Table
- Create PG sequence `chg_seq`
- Create `state_changelog` table (mirroring CHANGELOG.md fields: id, type, change_type, source, trigger, changed, why, verification, rollback, linked, framework_docs, category, created_at)
- `changelog-append.sh` uses `nextval("chg_seq")` for atomic CHG-ID generation
- Dual-write: PG (PRIMARY) + CHANGELOG.md (flat file, retains existing format)
- CHG entry also written to Notion (existing best-effort curl logic preserved)

## AC3: Cleanup & Hardening
- Add ID format validation to `ticket.sh create` (regex: ^TKT-\\d{4}$)
- CHANGELOG.md: migrate old-format entries (date-first `## 2026-... — [CHG-XXXX]`) to unified format (`## CHG-XXXX — Title`) so `changelog-append.sh` grep works consistently
- Verify `sequence` column in state_tickets: either repurpose for PG sequence tracking or document as deprecated
- Notion sync: add retry logic to `pg-to-notion-sync.sh` (currently fire-and-forget background `&`)
- Tickets.json dual-write: RETAINED — kept as backup/fallback per Ken directive
- Test chain: create ticket → verify PG + tickets.json + Notion. Create CHG → verify PG + CHANGELOG.md + Notion
acceptance_criteria: AC1: TKT Auto-Increment via PG Sequence — create ticket_seq, auto-generate in ticket.sh, validate format
AC2: CHG Auto-Increment via PG Sequence + state_changelog table — create chg_seq + state_changelog, atomic increment in changelog-append.sh
AC3: Cleanup — ID validation regex, CHANGELOG format unification, sequence column audit, Notion sync retry, tickets.json dual-write RETAINED

tickets.title: PG Write Path Silent Failure — db-write.sh reports OK but does not write

symptoms: psql accepts empty SQL as success
metadata.description: db-write.sh has a critical reliability gap: when Python crashes during SQL generation (e.g. SyntaxError from date strings like 08:25), the bash script does not check Python exit code. SQL variable is empty, psql -c empty succeeds (exit 0), and the script reports status ok but no data is written to PG. Root cause: (1) No Python exit code check after heredoc, (2) Empty SQL passes silently through psql, (3) No post-write verification. Also: the column is createdat (no underscore) but agents write created_at — db-write.sh routing to metadata is correct but the column name inconsistency causes failures. Audit scope: comprehensive test of all db-write.sh write paths (known columns, unknown→metadata, conflict/upsert, edge cases), post-write verification, and column name audit across all scripts that call db-write.sh. Deliverable: Fix + regression test.

tickets.title: Standardize PG Column Names — createdat vs created_at Schema Inconsistency

This is a platform-level schema inconsistency that causes silent data misplacement. Scope: (1) Audit all state_* tables for underscore-vs-non-underscore column name inconsistencies, (2) Decide: rename columns to underscore convention OR update all scripts to use current names, (3) Migrate existing NULL createdat from metadata JSONB, (4) Update all callers, (5) Add db-write.sh column alias mapping so future mismatches are caught at write time not silently rerouted. Deliverable: Clean, consistent column naming + no NULL createdat rows.

title: Pre-OC2 Mirror Isolation — Separate nexus_mirror from nexus_sandbox

metadata.description:
## Context
Mirror-writer polls live ainchors_nexus and upserts into shadow DB. Currently both mirror target AND test harness target are the SAME database (nexus_sandbox). Every test run writes test rows into shadow tables -> mirror-writer sees them as Extra noise -> WO-002 harness keeps flagging them. Co-location = signal permanently polluted.

## Decision (Ken + Atlas, 2026-06-10)
Option A: ISOLATE. Create nexus_mirror database on same PG instance (pre-OC2). At OC2, move to dedicated instance. Rejected Option B (filter test rows with _test_row flag) — compounding technical debt across every query/migration/report.

## Three Atlas Plan Corrections (Ken, 2026-06-10)
1. LONG-TERM = WAL STREAMING IS WRONG: Mirror is a LOGICAL PROJECTION through status_map to loop_plan/plan_atom, not byte-identical replication. WAL replica gives you state_tickets copy, not the transformed output the T4 harness needs. Poll-and-upsert stays. WAL/basebackup only when controller IS system of record for loop_plan.
2. RPO/RTO MISAPPLIED: Mirror is derived disposable state — reconcile-on-start rebuilds it in <1 interval. Formal DR targets for reconstructible data is over-engineering. Push to OC2 controller-of-record era.
3. THREE CONNECTION STRINGS IS CONFIG MUDDLE: Writer needs exactly 2 DSNs (live source + mirror target). Today NEXUS_DB_URL IS the mirror target. Isolation just repoints that one DSN from nexus_sandbox -> nexus_mirror. Do NOT add MIRROR_DB_URL.

## Implementation (4 steps, pre-OC2 only)
1. CREATE DATABASE nexus_mirror + run p0c001-p0c005 migrations against it
2. Repoint NEXUS_DB_URL in plist from nexus_sandbox -> nexus_mirror
3. Deploy daemon directly at nexus_mirror — first full sweep populates ~326 rows fresh from live
4. Add mirror_lag_seconds metric + document topology in ARCHITECTURE.md

## Why Before Clock Reset
Deploy against nexus_sandbox -> reset clock -> migrate to nexus_mirror later = reset twice, clock takes test-noise between. Isolation-first = one deploy, one target, clock starts clean and stays clean. Phase 3.1 (hand-delete 20 Extra test rows) becomes UNNECESSARY — test rows stay abandoned in nexus_sandbox where they belong.

## NOT IN SCOPE (pushed to OC2 epic)
- Dedicated OC2 instance for nexus_mirror
- Physical relocation to OC2
- WAL streaming / pg_basebackup
- RPO/RTO formalisation

## Acceptance Criteria
1. nexus_mirror DB exists with full shadow schema (p0c001-p0c005)
2. Mirror-writer daemon runs pointed at nexus_mirror, first sweep completed (~326 rows)
3. Test runs against nexus_sandbox produce ZERO Extra entries in WO-002 mismatch report
4. Topology documented in ARCHITECTURE.md with mirror_lag_seconds metric

## Sequencing
TKT-0395 (merge heartbeat PR) -> TKT-0396 (isolation) -> Phase 4 reconcile (clean) -> Phase 5 propagation -> reset clock -> Yoda bookkeeping

LINKED: TKT-0395, WO-002, TRIGGER-03 (OC2 — for future dedicated instance)

title: Auto-heal CHECK: audit all `:cloud` model crons with timeoutSeconds < 120

metadata.brief: L-087 root cause: yoda-context-brief-refresh (c69615bb) ran on gemma4:31b-cloud with timeoutSeconds:30 — an outlier. All comparable crons at :cloud models use 120s minimum (Forge Fallback Chain, PG-Notion Sync, Auto-Heal, Warden, TRIGGER-04/06 etc.). Cold-start + cloud latency + multi-file reads routinely exceed 30s. This TKT adds an auto-heal CHECK that scans every cron payload.model, and if it ends in :cloud AND payload.timeoutSeconds < 120, surfaces as NEEDS_KEN with the offending cron id/name/timeout/model. Prevents recurrence. Initial audit (2026-06-15): yoda-context-brief was the only outlier found, but a structural check is the right fix. Linked: CHG-0581 (the manual patch).
acceptance_criteria: New auto-heal CHECK scans all enabled crons via openclaw cron list --json. Filter: payload.kind==agentTurn AND payload.model ends in :cloud (systemEvent payloads have no model — skip those 11 crons).

For each matching cron where payload.timeoutSeconds < 120, add to needs_ken with: cron id, name, current timeout, model, suggested timeout. Suggested timeout is 120s default, 300s for daily sweeps, 600-831s for long-form gen — Forge to encode tier hint in alert text.

AC3b (review gate): Dry-run output surfaces to Ken via sovereign-alert.sh --source CRON_TIMEOUT_AUDIT. Ken reviews the list, approves any patches needed, then authorises flip-to-live.

AC3c (live): After Ken approval, CHECK goes live. Subsequent runs surface new offenders as Notion pages in DB B (Auto-Heal) per TKT-0401 pattern. Re-runs of fixed crons clear from needs_ken naturally.

Excludes EXPECTED_ERROR_CRONS and gateway-restart crons (same skip patterns as CHECK 29 in scripts/auto-heal.sh). Also excludes crons where payload.kind!=agentTurn (systemEvent, webhook) since they have no model/timeout.

metadata.resolution: scripts/pg-safe-start.sh wrapper added; homebrew.mxcl.postgresql@16.plist ProgramArguments updated to invoke /bin/bash scripts/pg-safe-start.sh <postgres-bin> -D <data-dir>.
